perturbation.changed_text: i) "Gross margin" is defined as gross revenue minus direct costs.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of "Gross margin" from a confidential definition (represented by [****]) to "gross revenue minus direct costs," we introduce ambiguity and potential disagreement over which costs are considered 'direct.' Other sections rely on this definition to calculate revenue sharing, and with a less precise definition, it becomes unclear exactly how these calculations should be made. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because without a clear definition, section 7(f)(iii) is less enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the Total Revenue earned by Sponsor during the three-month period exceeds the total of the Revenue Floor applicable to the same three-month period, Sponsor will pay Excite [****] of the gross margin Sponsor realizes on the gross revenue amount equal to the excess of the Total Revenue over the Revenue Floor during the three-month period.
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces a confidential definition of 'Gross margin' with a less precise one ('gross revenue minus direct costs'). The contradicted text refers to a percentage of the 'gross margin' for revenue sharing. The change introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes 'direct costs,' making the calculation of the gross margin, and therefore the revenue sharing, unclear and potentially unenforceable, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as essential language that provides clarity has been altered creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Sponsor will provide to Excite the content described in Exhibit A ("Content"), subject to the terms and conditions hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor will provide content to Excite, subject to the terms and conditions hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Exhibit A for the content, the definition of the content has been omitted, but the contract still requires 'Content' to be provided. This creates an internal contradiction because the agreement references specific 'Content', but with the removal of Exhibit A, the precise type and nature of the content is undefined. Section 4(b) becomes ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor will ensure that the Content will at all times feature the full array of content and functionality as made generally available by Sponsor at the Sponsor Site and its related Web sites, through any other means of distribution of Sponsor's own branded service or through any other third-party relationship, where Sponsor controls the Content.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that 'Content' was defined in Exhibit A. The changed text removes this reference, leaving the term 'Content' undefined. The contradicted text assumes a defined 'Content' by stating that it will feature the full array of content and functionality available at the Sponsor Site. Because the changed text removes the definition of 'Content', it creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, as the nature of the content to be provided is no longer clear. This aligns with the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each Usage Report will cover a calendar month and will be delivered within fifteen (15) days following the end of the applicable month.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Usage Report will cover a reporting period and will be delivered in a timely manner.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the frequency (calendar month to reporting period) and deadline (within fifteen days to a timely manner), the provision becomes too vague. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 8(d) because the accuracy of usage reports is warranted only to the extent that such reports serve as the basis for payments. Without a defined reporting period, the basis for calculating payments is vague.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SPONSOR AND EXCITE WILL USE REASONABLE EFFORTS TO ENSURE THE ACCURACY OF THE USAGE REPORTS BUT NEITHER PARTY WARRANTS THAT THE USAGE REPORTS WILL CONFORM TO ANY SPECIFICATIONS AT ANY GIVEN TIME. NEITHER PARTY WILL BE HELD LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIMS AS THEY RELATE TO SUCH USAGE REPORTS, EXCEPT TO THE EXTENT THAT SUCH USAGE REPORTS SERVE AS THE BASIS FOR PAYMENTS UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, by replacing a specific timeframe for usage report delivery (within fifteen days) with a vague "timely manner," introduces significant ambiguity. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that usage reports serve as the basis for payments. The change makes it unclear when these reports should be delivered, potentially impacting the accuracy and reliability of payment calculations. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction because the basis for calculating payments is now vague, jeopardizing the intended link between payments and the usage reports warranted accuracy.
